content: Honestly, this is the absolute limit. I've been waiting for my delivery since Tuesday, and now you're telling me it's lost in transit? I want a full refund immediately.
I honestly cannot thank you enough! The flowers arrived absolutely beautiful, and my mum was over the moon with them. Exceptional service!
Could you please let me know what time the next train to London Paddington leaves? I think there might be a bank holiday timetable in place.
The new layout of the app is quite disappointing, to be honest. It's much harder to find the search bar now, and it keeps lagging when I scroll.
That recipe you suggested worked a treat last night. The roast potatoes were lovely and crispy, so I'll definitely be making them again.
I need to reschedule our meeting on Thursday afternoon because a prior engagement has overrun. Would next Wednesday at 10 am suit you instead?
My boiler has completely broken down and there is water leaking all over the kitchen lino. Please send an emergency plumber out as soon as possible, it's freezing.
Cheers for sending over that spreadsheet. Looks fine to me at a quick glance, I'll have a proper look tomorrow morning.
I am just setting up my new smart speaker now. Do I need to download the companion app on my mobile first, or can I do it via Bluetooth?
It's a bit of a nuisance that the local shop has stopped stocking those gluten-free biscuits. I suppose I'll have to order them online now.
We've just had the test results back and it's completely clear! I can't even begin to describe the weight that has been lifted off our shoulders.
Please be aware that the main server migration will commence at midnight tonight. Ensure all your local files are backed up to the cloud before you finish for the day.
I've been on hold for forty minutes just to change my billing address. Surely there's a simpler way to do this online without having to queue?
I had a brilliant chat with Sarah from your support team earlier today. She was incredibly patient with me and sorted my account issue out in no time.
I'm trying to decide whether to book the holiday cottage in Cornwall or the one in Pembrokeshire. Both look quite nice but the Welsh one is slightly cheaper.
I'm absolutely devastated to hear about Uncle Arthur's passing. It feels completely surreal, and we are all struggling to process the news.
The weather forecast says it might actually stay dry this afternoon. Shall we pop down to the pub garden for a quick pint after work?
That film was incredibly dull, to be quite frank. It dragged on for nearly three hours and the ending didn't make a lick of sense.
Do you know if the rubbish bins are being collected on their usual day this week, or is it delayed because of the bank holiday?
I've just bought tickets to see my absolute favourite band at the O2 Arena next year! I literally cannot wait, this is going to be the best night ever!
I'm starting to get a bit worried about the dog because he hasn't touched his food all day and seems very lethargic. I might have to ring the vet if he doesn't improve by evening.
We need to finalise the mortgage valuation report by Friday if we want to exchange contracts on time. Has the surveyor sent over the paperwork yet?
The new winter coat arrived yesterday and it fits perfectly. The material is lovely and thick, so it should keep me quite warm during the commute.
I think my credit card details have been stolen because there are three massive transactions on my statement that I definitely didn't authorise. Please freeze my account immediately.
Just letting you know that I've parked the car on the driveway. The keys are hanging on the hook by the front door.
Honestly, I can't thank you enough for sorting out the flat's heating so quickly. My mum is staying over and she was absolutely freezing, so this is a massive relief.
I am absolutely fuming about the delivery charge. You promised next-day delivery and it's been four days now, but you still charged me the premium rate!
I think the weather is supposed to clear up by Tuesday anyway. Doesn't really matter to me as I'll be working indoors all week.
The new layout of the local Sainsbury's is actually quite nice now that I've got used to it. It takes a bit less time to find the bakery section.
Just so you know, the bathroom tap is still dripping slightly even after I tightened it. It's not urgent, but we should probably get a plumber to look at it before it worsens.
Had a brilliant chat with Sarah today at the pub. She's doing really well after her promotion and sends her love.
My laptop has completely crashed right in the middle of writing my dissertation introduction. I haven't backed up any of today's work and I'm honestly panicking.
The train departs from platform 4 at 14:15. We'll need to leave the flat by half past one if we want to catch it without rushing.
The cafe was out of oat milk again this morning, which is a bit of a nuisance. I just ended up getting a black Americano instead.
We just got the news that our mortgage application has been approved! I'm absolutely over the moon, I can't believe we're actually buying our first home.
Here is the contact number for the dentist in Cardiff: 029 2011 2233. They said they have an opening for a check-up next Thursday afternoon.
I'm really quite disappointed with the quality of this jumper. After just one wash at thirty degrees, the seams have started to unravel.
That new Italian place down the high street does the most incredible sourdough pizza. We absolutely must go there next weekend!
I think I did reasonably well in the interview today. The panel seemed quite friendly and they asked some really interesting questions about my previous project.
I've been trying to get through to the GP surgery for over an hour and the line just keeps cutting out. This is completely unacceptable when trying to book an urgent appointment.
I'll be working from home on Thursday, but I'll be in the London office for the rest of the week if you need to catch up in person.
Oh, cheers for sending over those minutes from the team meeting. I'll have a quick browse through them before the call tomorrow.
I'm not particularly keen on the new colour scheme they've chosen for the living room. It feels a bit too dark and gloomy compared to the old cream paint.
My bank card has been cloned and someone has just spent £400 at an electronics shop in Manchester. I need to freeze my account immediately!
The recycling bin is collected on alternate Wednesdays, so we need to make sure we put it out by the kerb tonight.
The package just arrived and the packaging is thankfully fully recyclable, which is a nice touch. The items inside look to be in perfect condition.
I've had the worst day at work; my manager completely ignored my suggestions during the presentation and took all the credit himself. I'm honestly tempted to hand in my notice.
It's starting to drizzle outside, which means my walk to the station is going to be slightly damp. I should have brought my brolly.
I managed to find those vintage records you wanted at a car boot sale in Bristol. They're in pretty decent nick too, barely any scratches.
I am so incredibly proud of Lucy for passing her driving test on the first go! She has worked so hard for this over the last six months.
